name: InfluxDB Python Client (Time-Series Database)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); InfluxData; InfluxDB Cloud serverless or on-prem

performance.notes: Line protocol optimized for high-throughput writes; fast time-range queries

agent_readiness.notes: Python client for InfluxDB time-series database. from influxdb_client import InfluxDBClient, Point. client = InfluxDBClient(url='http://localhost:8086', token=token, org=org). write_api.write(bucket, org, Point('measurement').tag('host', 'srv1').field('cpu', 85.5)). query_api.query('from(bucket: "metrics") |> range(start: -1h)'). Flux query language (v2) or SQL (v3). Pandas integration. Async writes with batching. Line protocol for high-throughput. InfluxDB 3 uses Apache Arrow/Parquet.

gotchas: InfluxDB 2.x (Flux) vs 3.x (SQL) have different APIs and clients
Flux query language has steep learning curve
InfluxDB 3 is major architectural change (IOx engine)
best_when: IoT sensor telemetry and device metrics
When InfluxDB-specific ecosystem (Telegraf, Chronograf) in use
High-throughput write workloads via line protocol
avoid_when: SQL joins and relational queries (TimescaleDB better)
Teams preferring SQL over Flux query language
summary: InfluxDB Python client writes and queries time-series data. Point('measurement').tag('host', 'srv1').field('cpu', 85.5) via write_api. Flux or SQL queries via query_api. Async batch writes for high throughput. Line protocol for IoT ingestion. Pandas integration. InfluxDB 3 (IOx, Arrow/Parquet) is new architecture — check client version compatibility. Telegraf ecosystem for metric collection.
